author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
AUDIT FINDINGS:

CLEAN:
- Zero TODOs/FIXMEs/unimplemented!() in core, gpu, optim (1 comment-only TODO in nn/dropout.rs)
- Zero compilation warnings across entire workspace
- All backward functions have GPU dispatch for f32 (CPU fallback only for f64/no-backend)
- Shape backward ops (reshape, squeeze, transpose) correctly use zero-copy views, no GPU dispatch needed
- Storage panics (as_slice on GPU) are documented API contracts, not bugs
- Clone impl panics in storage.rs are acceptable since Clone can't return Result — try_clone() exists as the safe alternative

REMAINING CPU ROUND-TRIPS (acceptable, not bugs):
- expand() forward: downloads to CPU for broadcast indexing, re-uploads (rare op)
- cat/split backward: CPU round-trip via ensure_cpu (low-frequency in training)
- cumulative ops (cumsum, cumprod): CPU fallback in forward+backward
- mean_dim(): forward now GPU-dispatched (CL-338), backward still CPU
- elementwise::mean() for non-f32: CPU fallback

NOT IMPLEMENTED (trait defaults returning errors):
- f64 GPU ops (add_f64, mul_f64, etc.) — 7 trait methods
- GPU linalg: svd_f32, cholesky_f32, solve_f32, qr_f32
- These are intentional — f64 GPU and decompositions are low priority

NO BUGS FOUND. Codebase is clean.
